subject: Chemistry
title: Novichok: The Russian Nerve Agent
description: A short lesson to help you apply Novichok: The Russian Nerve Agent.

hook.text: In 2018, a former Russian spy and his daughter collapsed on a park bench in Salisbury, England.

buildup.text: They were poisoned with Novichok—a nerve agent 5 to 10 times more potent than sarin.

discovery.text: Like sarin, Novichok blocks acetylcholinesterase. But it binds more tightly and is harder to reverse.

twist.text: The poison was smeared on a door handle. A police officer who investigated also became seriously ill.

climax.text: Months later, a woman died after her partner found a discarded perfume bottle containing Novichok in a bin.

punchline.text: A nerve agent that lingers on surfaces for months. Deadlier than sarin.

quiz.question: How was Novichok delivered in the 2018 Salisbury poisoning?
options: Smeared on the front door handle of the victims' home
Injected with a poisoned umbrella tip
